text.title: IaC Module Design Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `IaC Module Design Patterns`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Module input variables MUST be the minimal set the caller needs; expose internal knobs only when there is a documented use-case.
rule.rationale: Large input surface increases miscalibration risk + slows upgrades.

rule.statement: Every output MUST declare a type (or equivalent) + description; untyped outputs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Untyped outputs break downstream consumers silently on schema drift.

rule.statement: Each module MUST ship at least one runnable example under examples/ that terraform validate passes.
rule.rationale: Without runnable examples, consumers reverse-engineer usage from tests.

rule.statement: Module releases MUST be SemVer-tagged in git; floating main references are not stable consumption surface.
rule.rationale: SemVer encodes upgrade safety promise; floating refs break it.

rule.statement: Removing or renaming an input/output MUST go through one major version with deprecation warning before removal.
rule.rationale: Hard breaks cascade to every consumer; deprecation cycle absorbs the migration cost.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
